findings.evidence: `rg -n "mem_OSet_rotate|mem_XSet_rotate" code/TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid` has no hits; the parallel API exists for transpose at `Diagram.lean:851` and `Diagram.lean:859`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` lemmas matching the existing transpose API: `p ∈ G.rotate.OSet ↔ Prod.map Fin.rev Fin.rev p ∈ G.OSet` and the analogous `XSet` lemma.
findings.issue: `GridDiagram.rotate` lacks the membership eliminators for rotated marking sets (`mem_OSet_rotate` and `mem_XSet_rotate`), so users only get `rotate_OSet`/`rotate_XSet` and must rewrite through `Finset.image`.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "J_rotate|JO_rotate|JX_rotate" code/TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid` has no hits; the existing transpose symmetry exposes these characteristic lemmas at `JFunction.lean:477`, `JFunction.lean:506`, and `JFunction.lean:511`.
findings.fix: Add the specialized lemmas `GridState.J x.rotate y.rotate = GridState.J x y`, `G.rotate.JO x.rotate = G.JO x`, and `G.rotate.JX x.rotate = G.JX x`, proved via `rotate_pointSet`, `rotate_OSet`/`rotate_XSet`, and `GridPoint.J_image_rev`.
findings.issue: The public `J`-function API is incomplete for the new symmetry: there are no state/diagram-level lemmas `GridState.J_rotate`, `GridDiagram.JO_rotate`, or `GridDiagram.JX_rotate`, despite exposing the lower-level `GridPoint.J_image_rev`.

summary: The rotation definitions expose the basic set images and grading invariance, but the characteristic public API is not complete enough to use the new symmetry without unfolding through point sets.
